Chapter: Handbook And Workspace Stewardship.
Current document status: Needs Leadership Review.

Section: Handbook And Workspace Stewardship Guide.

Section: Purpose.

This guide keeps the financial operations handbook, current workroom, and church record maintainable as officers, committee members, volunteers, and staff change. The system should be church-owned work, not a private project that depends on one person.

Section: What Belongs Where.

Table summary.
Information: Durable governance, policy, role, or procedure. Proper Home: Handbook. Why: It should remain useful after current people and projects change..
Information: Assigned task, due date, or recurring work. Proper Home: Portal checklist, current dashboard, or approved task tracker. Why: Work should have a visible owner and next action rather than being buried in narrative..
Information: Question awaiting a decision. Proper Home: Issue register or committee workspace. Why: It is working material, not settled guidance..
Information: Committee or congregational decision. Proper Home: Decision log and authoritative minutes where applicable. Why: Future leaders need the decision, date, owner, and approval source..
Information: Review packet, implementation note, or evaluation. Proper Home: Current Workroom. Why: It supports a time-limited project and should be archived when complete..
Information: Approved or superseded release. Proper Home: Church record / archive. Why: The history remains available without competing with current guidance..

Google Drive or another restricted system may remain the proper home for editable office files and confidential records. The public handbook should link to safe source material only when useful.

Section: Shared Responsibilities.

Table summary.
Responsibility: Document owner. Assigned To: Committee, officer, or church body responsible for the subject. Meaning: Decides what the document should say and requests review or approval..
Responsibility: Documentation steward. Assigned To: One or more trusted helpers. Meaning: Maintains formatting, links, metadata, status labels, navigation, and archives without changing policy on their own..
Responsibility: Reviewer. Assigned To: People affected by or knowledgeable about the document. Meaning: Tests clarity, accuracy, practicality, and alignment with other documents..
Responsibility: Approval body. Assigned To: Committee, leadership body, or congregation with authority. Meaning: Approves the document when required and establishes its effective date..
Responsibility: Church Clerk / records steward. Assigned To: As assigned by church practice. Meaning: Preserves authoritative minutes, motions, and approved versions..

Section: Document Lifecycle.

Working Draft: an owner is developing the content.
Needs Review: the appropriate committee, leadership body, bylaw reviewer, or professional is reviewing it.
Ready for Church Consideration: committee review is complete and congregational action is required.
Approved / Current: the authorized body approved it and the effective date is recorded.
Superseded: a newer approved version replaced it; the old version remains in the archive.
Parking Lot: useful future work that is not an active priority.

Review packets, issue registers, implementation roadmaps, and evaluation notes are current-work records. They should not be labeled as approved policies simply because they contain useful analysis.

Section: Minimum Metadata.

Every document in the portal catalog should identify:

Document type.
Status.
Owner.
Intended audiences.
Lifecycle category.
Approval body and effective date once approved.
Next review date for policies, procedures, charters, and role descriptions.

Audience labels organize the reading experience. They do not make public repository content confidential.

Section: Change Process.

A reader submits feedback or contacts the document owner.
A documentation steward records or routes the request.
The owner decides whether the change is editorial, operational, policy-related, or governance-related.
Editorial corrections may be prepared directly; substantive changes return to the proper reviewer or approval body.
The decision and resulting status are recorded.
Approved changes are published, and any replaced version is marked superseded.

Section: Review Rhythm.
